title: ACP zombie processes — Close() doesn't reliably kill subprocesses
description:
ACP agent subprocesses accumulate across runs. The 5s kill timeout in acpSession.Close() doesn't clean up all processes. After several tk run sessions, dozens of orphaned node claude-agent-acp processes remain.

Root cause: Close() sends SIGKILL after 5s, but the npm parent process and child node process are separate PIDs. Killing the node process may leave the npm wrapper alive, or vice versa.

Fix: kill the entire process group (negative PID to kill) or use proc.Process.Signal + process group kill.
closed_reason: Fixed: set Setpgid on subprocess, kill entire process group (-pid) on Close(). Verified: 0 zombies after session close.
